questions.question: Consider a distributed system where processes P1, P2, and P3 are connected through a network. Initially, process P1 thinks it is at logical time 0. Process P2 thinks it is at logical time 3. Process P3 thinks it is at logical time 2. Determine which process is clocked behind and how they can synchronize their clocks. (10 marks)

A distributed system consists of two processes, P1 and P2. Process P1 thinks it is at logical time 10 and has sent a message to process P2. Process P2, which is clocked at the same logical time as P1, receives the message after 2 time units. What is the relative speed of the message and the clock? (8 marks)

In a distributed system, clock synchronization is achieved through a protocol that involves periodically sending a 'synchronization message' from a centralized clock to all other nodes. If a node receives the message and is clocked 5 time units behind the sender, how long should it wait before re-sending the message to synchronize its clock? (6 marks)
